'''Kevin John Brown''' (born 11 October 1984) is a former English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|so}} for the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]]. He has played for [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He played as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]], and captained both the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]]. He played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the top flight, and on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[2019 Betfred Championship]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Club career==
===Early career===
Born in [[St Helens, Merseyside|St Helens]], Brown played junior rugby for several teams, including [[Thatto Heath Crusaders]], Haydock Warriors, [[Pilkington Recs]] and [[Blackbrook A.R.L.F.C.|Blackbrook]].<ref>{{cite web|last1=Turner|first1=Matt|title=Warrington Wolves stand-off Kevin Brown speaks at length about his hopes for his time at the club|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/15089611.Winning_trophy_with_Wolves_would__complete__Brown_s_career|website=Warrington Guardian|access-date=10 December 2017|date=14 February 2017}}</ref> Although he initially struggled to impress scouts, he was signed by [[Wigan Warriors]] at the age of 16.<ref>{{cite web|title=Rugby League World Exclusive – Kevin Brown|url=http://www.totalrl.com/rugby-league-world-exclusive-kevin-brown|website=Total Rugby League|publisher=League Publications|access-date=10 December 2017|date=17 November 2015}}</ref> Brown played for Wigan's academy side in 2002 and 2003.

Brown was called up into the 2002 England Academy squad that beat the Australian Schoolboys in winter 2002. He had originally been included in the stand-by squad, but got his chance following the failure of Hull centre [[Kirk Yeaman]] to recover from a shoulder injury.

Brown was also selected for the Super League U21s squad to face the National League U21s in June 2003. Shortly afterwards, Wigan acknowledged the player's tremendous potential by extending his contract until the end of 2005. Wigan coach [[Stuart Raper]] commented: "I am delighted Kevin has re-signed as he is a big part of my future plans."{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

===Wigan Warriors===
In April 2003, due to a significant number of first team injuries, Brown was one of several academy players to make their senior début against his hometown team [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], appearing as a substitute in a 24–22 victory over St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|title=Memory Lane: Wigan v St. Helens|url=http://wiganwarriors.com/news/2005-03-23-memory-lane-wigan-v-st-helens|publisher=Wigan Warriors|access-date=10 December 2017|date=23 March 2005|archive-date=10 December 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171210232347/http://wiganwarriors.com/news/2005-03-23-memory-lane-wigan-v-st-helens|url-status=dead}}</ref> He scored his first tries later in the season with a hat-trick against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]].

Brown became a first team regular during the 2004's [[Super League IX]], playing almost every game until he fractured his [[fibula]] (leg bone) in a match against the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Brown blow for Wigan|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/3746171.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=10 December 2017|date=25 May 2004}}</ref> Despite injury and shaky form,{{citation needed|date=February 2020}} he signed a new deal in July 2005 keeping him at the Wigan Warriors until the end of the 2007's [[Super League XII]].

Newly appointed [[head coach]] [[Ian Millward]] commented: "Kevin is still only 20 and a very exciting player. He is still improving and as he gets better, which I am sure he will, he will be an important part of our attack."
[[File:Kevin Brown Wigan.JPG|thumb|right|300px|Brown playing for Huddersfield]]

===Huddersfield Giants===
In June 2006, Brown joined [[Huddersfield Giants]] on loan until the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Huddersfield seal Brown loan deal|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/huddersfield/5128794.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=10 December 2017|date=29 June 2006}}</ref>

On 19 August 2006, Brown made the deal permanent, signing a three-year contract with the club.<ref>{{cite web|title=Huddersfield secure Brown signing|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/huddersfield/5267420.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=10 December 2017|date=19 August 2006}}</ref> In 2009, Brown signed a new three-year contract, keeping him at the club until 2012.<ref>{{cite web|title=Brown pens new Huddersfield deal|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/huddersfield/8153461.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=10 December 2017|date=16 July 2009}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings===
In May 2012, it was announced that Brown had signed a four-year contract with [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] from the 2013 season for an undisclosed fee.<ref>{{cite web|title=Kevin Brown to leave Huddersfield Giants for Widnes|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18221770|website=BBC Sport|access-date=10 December 2017|date=26 May 2012}}</ref> He was named at stand-off in the 2014 [[Super League Dream Team]], and was the first ever Widnes player to be selected in the team.<ref>{{cite web|title=Kevin Brown Named In 2014 Dream Team|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/news/article/40548/kevin-brown-named-in|publisher=Widnes Vikings|access-date=10 December 2017|date=15 September 2014|archive-date=10 December 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171210232341/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/news/article/40548/kevin-brown-named-in|url-status=dead}}</ref> Brown was also named as captain for the 2015 season following the retirement of previous captain [[Jon Clarke (rugby league)|Jon Clarke]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Kevin Brown: Widnes Vikings captain hopes to lead by example|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/30227925|website=BBC Sport|access-date=10 December 2017|date=27 November 2014}}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves===
In December 2016 it was announced that Brown had signed a two-year contract with [[Warrington Wolves]] for an undisclosed fee.<ref>{{cite web|title=Kevin Brown: Widnes Vikings stand-off joins Warrington Wolves|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/38265391|website=BBC Sport|access-date=10 December 2017|date=9 December 2016}}</ref>

He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>
[[File:Kev Brown BBC.jpg|thumb|right|Brown working as a pundit for the BBC at the 2021 RLWC]]

===Leigh Centurions===
Brown spent time on loan from Warrington at the [[Leigh Centurions]] in 2019.

===Salford===
On 17 October 2020, he played in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat for Salford against Leeds at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

In round 4 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored two tries in a 34-8 victory over Leigh.   It was Salford's first win of the year after the club had lost the opening three games.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/salford-vs-leigh/report/55439/|title=Red Devils beat Centurions for first win of 2021 season|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

==International career==
In June 2007 Brown was called up to the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] squad for the [[Test match (rugby league)|Test match]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6744909.stm|title=Eight new faces in Lions squad|publisher=BBC|date=2007-06-12|access-date=2007-06-12}}</ref>

He was selected to play for England against France in the one-off test in 2010.<ref>{{cite news 
|last = Hadfield
|first = Dave
|title = Widdop passes his first Test at a canter
|work = The Independent
|place =UK
|publisher = Independent Print Limited
|date = 2010-06-13
|url = https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/widdop-passes-his-first-test-at--a-canter-1999087.html
|access-date = 2010-07-08}}</ref> He was also a part of England's [[2010 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] campaign later in the year.

In October 2016, Brown was selected in the England squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]]. He returned to the international scene where he featured in a [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations#France vs England|test match]] against France, scoring his first international try in the process, in his country's 40-6 win.

Brown was a surprise inclusion in England's [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|2017 World Cup]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Shaw|first1=Matthew|title=Kevin Brown a shock-call up for England|url=http://www.totalrl.com/kevin-brown-shock-call-england|website=Total Rugby League|access-date=10 December 2017}}</ref><ref name="England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-roby-back-fold-wayne-11312505|title=England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold|publisher=Mirror|date=9 October 2017|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref> He was not selected for the first two group games, with [[Gareth Widdop]] and [[Luke Gale]] being preferred as the starting halfbacks. Brown has given his first start in the final group game against France, with Widdop moving to the {{rlp|fb}} position.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Bower|first1=Aaron|title=England's Kevin Brown: 'I know my first World Cup game could be my last'|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/nov/11/kevin-brown-warrington-england-world-cup-chance|website=The Observer|publisher=Guardian News and Media|access-date=10 December 2017|date=11 November 2017}}</ref> He retained his place for the 36–6 quarter final victory over [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]], but was substituted at half time after suffering a concussion.<ref>{{cite web|title=Rugby League World Cup: England player spots Kevin Brown knockout on social media|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/42044086|website=BBC Sport|access-date=10 December 2017|date=19 November 2017}}</ref> He was cleared to play in time for the semi-final against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]],<ref>{{cite web|title=CONFIRMATION: Kevin Brown will play for England against Tonga in Rugby League World Cup 2017 semi final|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/15682116.CONFIRMATION__Brown_will_play_for_England_against_Tonga_in_Rugby_League_World_Cup_2017_semi_final|website=Warrington Guardian|access-date=10 December 2017|date=24 November 2017}}</ref> helping England win 20–18 to reach the World Cup final for the first time in 22 years. Brown started once again at [[Five-eighth|stand off]] in the final against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]], which England went on to lose 0–6.

==Post-playing career==
Brown has worked as a pundit for the [[BBC]] since hanging up his boots.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}
